body:
## What's Changed

`0.41.1` is a polish patch on top of `0.41.0`'s boot-perf overhaul. The branch / tag sidebar cursor sync is live again (was firing inconsistently due to a debounce that swallowed fast scrolls), the compose view's right panel now shows counts on its `Staged` / `Unstaged` section headers to match the status surface, and the worktrees view gains a chained "remove worktree + delete branch" action so the canonical "I'm done with this side branch" wind-down is one keystroke instead of three.

### Highlights

- **Counts on compose Staged / Unstaged section headers.** The compose view's right-hand Worktree panel previously rendered `Staged` / `Unstaged` as bare labels while the status surface (post-#791) shows `▾ Staged (n)` / `▾ Unstaged (n)`. Visual language now matches across surfaces. Counts come from the worktree overview totals so the section header doesn't lie when there are more files than the 12-row visible slice can show.
- **`D` on the worktrees view removes worktree + deletes the branch.** Two delete actions now live in the worktrees view: `W` keeps its existing "remove worktree only" semantics, and `D` chains `git worktree remove` → `git branch -d` so the user doesn't have to remove the worktree, navigate to the branches view, and delete the branch separately. Both pre-flight guards inherit from the underlying helpers (refuses current/dirty worktrees; uses safe `-d` so unmerged branches don't lose work). Per-view scoping in the input dispatcher means `D` from any other surface keeps doing what it always did.

### Fixes

- Fixed the branch / tag sidebar cursor sync feeling inconsistent. The 0.39.0 auto-jump was wrapped in a 150ms trailing-edge debounce: a fast scroll through a long branch list cancelled the timer on every keystroke and only fired once on release, so the user never saw the history graph cursor follow their navigation. Switched to synchronous fire-on-effect with a ref-based dedup so the cursor moves in real time and adjacent branches that share a tip don't flood the status line with redundant `Synced…` messages.

### PRs Included

- #841 Make branch/tag sidebar cursor sync feel live (closes #839)
- #842 Show counts on compose Staged / Unstaged section headers (closes #840)
- #843 `D` on the worktrees view removes worktree + branch (closes #838)

### Breaking Changes

None known. The `D` keystroke on the worktrees view was previously falling through to the global `delete-branch` workflow (which would target whatever branch was last cursored on the branches view rather than acting on the worktree under the cursor). The new behavior intercepts `D` only when the user is actually on the worktrees surface with worktrees in view, so any prior reliance on the previous fallthrough was almost certainly accidental.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.41.0...0.41.1

summary: `0.41.0` is a boot-perf overhaul of `coco ui` and `coco log -i`, paired with a visual-polish pass on the surfaces that the earlier 0.40.x sprints left rough. The pre-mount black-terminal stretch durin
body:
## What's Changed

`0.41.0` is a boot-perf overhaul of `coco ui` and `coco log -i`, paired with a visual-polish pass on the surfaces that the earlier 0.40.x sprints left rough. The pre-mount black-terminal stretch during the synchronous `git log` is gone, the workstation chrome paints in the first frame, subsequent boots in the same repo render history immediately from a per-repo disk cache, and per-keystroke work in the runtime collapses into memoized lookups. On top of that, the `?` help overlay actually fits its descriptions, history rows stop wrapping onto a second visual line, full-graph lane-closure rows fade as scaffolding instead of looking like missing commits, and the branches / tags / worktrees promoted views render with aligned columns regardless of name length.

## Highlights

### Instant TUI mount

- The runtime no longer awaits `git log` before mounting Ink. The workstation chrome paints in under 100ms with `· Loading commits…` in the history surface and a `loading commits` hint in the top header. When the fetch returns, rows swap in transparently.
- The mounted-ref guard prevents a late-resolving promise from dispatching after the user `q` quits before rows arrive.
- Failure path: a failed boot fetch surfaces the error in the status line and clears the placeholder so the user knows something went wrong.
- Non-TTY callers (CI logs, piped output) still resolve the loader synchronously before handing rows to the static formatter, so `coco log` stdout / JSON output is unchanged.

### Stale-while-revalidate disk cache

- Per-repo JSON cache of the most recent successful `git log` result. Subsequent boots in the same repo paint the history view with cached commits in the first frame while the fresh fetch runs in the background.
- Cache lives at `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/coco/overview/` (falls back to `~/.cache/coco/overview/`), keyed by `sha1(repoPath)` so two repos at different paths never collide.
- Hard cap at 500 rows per cache entry to bound disk size.
- Best-effort: read failures, schema mismatches, and bad JSON all return `undefined` and boot falls through to the loading placeholder. Write failures are swallowed silently. The cache is never load-bearing.

### Lazy `pullRequest` fetch

- The full PR overview (`gh pr view --json` with the enriched field set) no longer fires on every boot. The slim `provider.currentPullRequest` field already covers every boot consumer (header glyph, yank handler, workflow runner); the dedicated PR view is the only surface that needs the heavier data and now triggers its own load on entry.
- Net effect: one fewer `gh pr view` round-trip on every boot. Users who never open the PR view never pay for the heavier fetch at all.

### Per-keystroke work memoization

- `parseStashDiffFiles` previously ran 2-3x per keystroke when the user was on a stash diff (input dispatch + yank handler + render). Now memoized at the runtime level: single parse per stash patch open, shared across all consumers.
- Promoted-view filtered lists (branches / tags / stashes / worktrees) previously rebuilt inline inside the `useInput` callback on every keystroke. Now memoized on `(raw list, filter)` so the input dispatcher reads `.length` off pre-derived arrays instead of re-running `.filter(matchesPromotedFilter)` per arrow-key press.
- No functional change. Visible win is fewer wasted CPU cycles per keystroke on busy repos and large stashes.

### Help overlay reads in full

- Pressing `?` previously rendered the hotkey list into the inspector's at-rest narrow width, truncating every action description to "Move focus...", "Push the d...", and so on. Users could see the keys but not what they did.
- The right panel now expands to ~50% of the terminal (clamped to 60-100 cells) while the help overlay is open. Descriptions render in full; the override wins over both the at-rest and inspector-focused widths so the behavior is the same regardless of which focus the user came from.

### Single-line history rows

- `renderCommitHistoryRow` previously truncated to a hardcoded 140-cell budget. When the actual main panel was narrower than 140 (any time the inspector or sidebar was focused, on the wider help-overlay panel that ships in the same release, or on smaller terminals generally), row content overflowed and Ink wrapped onto a second visual line. The next commit's graph indicator then landed against the wrap continuation rather than its own commit.
- Truncation now uses the actual panel width. Refs trail the message and shrink first when the row is narrow, since the user can always see the full ref list in the inspector, so the headline subject keeps priority over decoration.

### Full-graph lane-closure rows fade as scaffolding

- In full graph mode, `git --graph` emits lane-closure rows (`|/`, `|\`, etc.) between commits to indicate where branches converge. They previously rendered at the same color weight as commit rows, so the eye read them as blank rows where a commit was skipped.
- Force-dim graph-only rows so they retreat as topology connectors rather than competing with commit content. Both the outer `Text` and the inner per-lane segment spans get the dim treatment so the lane colors don't override the parent's "this whole row should fade" intent.

### Aligned columns in promoted views

- Branches, tags, and worktrees views previously padded their name / branch columns to a hardcoded width (28 cells for branches and worktrees, 20 for tags). Short rows left a wide gutter; long names overflowed and pushed every following column rightward out of alignment.
- Column widths now derive from the longest entry in the visible window, capped at 40 cells so one runaway long label doesn't blow the trailing columns off-screen. Truncate long entries inside the column and pad short entries out to match: both sides converge so the timestamp / subject / state column lands in the same spot on every row.

## Notable

- **The first boot in a repo is the same speed as before.** The disk cache only pays off on the second and subsequent boots; the first run still does the full `git log` from scratch (and writes the cache for next time).
- **The PR view's first entry now shows a brief loading state.** The full PR data was previously eager-loaded on boot for every user; now it's lazy. Users who open `g p` will see a short "loading" while the enriched fetch lands; everyone else sees a faster boot.
- **Cached commits may be slightly stale during the refresh window.** The header's `loading commits` indicator flags this; once the fresh fetch lands, the rows update in place. Manual `r` and the live fs-watcher continue to keep the cache fresh after the boot.
- **Long branch / tag / worktree names truncate inside their column.** Previously they overflowed and pushed neighbors rightward; now they get a tail ellipsis at the column boundary. The full name still appears in the inspector / preview panel.

## PRs Included

**Boot perf overhaul (#808).** The four PRs in this sprint together close #808's first wave.

- #826 Defer commit-log fetch into runtime for instant boot
- #827 Lazy-load full pullRequest until PR view entry
- #828 Per-repo disk cache of the last commit-log fetch
- #829 Hoist per-keystroke duplicate work into useMemo

**Visual polish across surfaces.** Four UX papercuts the boot work surfaced.

- #834 Expand right panel when help overlay is active (closes #832)
- #835 Truncate history rows to actual panel width (closes #830)
- #836 Dim graph-only lane-closure rows so they recede (closes #831)
- #837 Aligned columns for branches / tags / worktrees views (closes #833)

## Breaking Changes

None known. The disk cache is opt-in via the existing entry points and falls back cleanly when missing or unreadable. The lazy PR fetch is invisible to anyone who doesn't open the PR view; PR view consumers just see a short loading state on first entry instead of the data being pre-warmed. The visual changes are all additive: longer subjects truncate where they used to wrap, columns align where they used to be ragged, the help panel takes more width while it's open, and graph-only rows fade where they used to render full-strength.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.40.1...0.41.0

summary: `0.40.1` is a patch release that recovers `coco ui` from a boot-time crash introduced by the `0.40.0` inspector work, makes the inspector's tab navigation visible and easier to discover, and surfaces
body:
## What's Changed

`0.40.1` is a patch release that recovers `coco ui` from a boot-time crash introduced by the `0.40.0` inspector work, makes the inspector's tab navigation visible and easier to discover, and surfaces error messages on command failures without requiring `--verbose`.

### Highlights

- Inspector tab indicator (`[Inspector] Actions · ←/→ switch`) now renders in both tall (stacked) and short-terminal (tabbed) layouts. Previously it only appeared on short terminals, leaving tall-terminal users with no visible signal that the actions list was cursor-targetable.
- `←` / `→` swap inspector tabs, mirroring the sidebar's left/right tab axis. `[` and `]` keep working as alternates so existing muscle memory carries.
- Inspector enforces a single-cursor invariant: the file list's selection highlight is suppressed when the actions tab is active (and vice versa), so the cursor lives in exactly one section at a time.
- Generic command errors print their cause line unconditionally on failure. The `Failed to execute command` banner used to hide the actual error message behind `--verbose`, which is also consumed by `yarn` and `npm` wrappers and never reaches the script. The `--verbose` flag still controls the full stack trace.

### Fixes

- Fixed `coco ui` crashing on startup with `Text string " " must be rendered inside <Text> component`. The inspector tab header had raw whitespace strings as direct `Box` children, which Ink rejects. The crash fired on every boot of the history view's inspector after `0.40.0` shipped.
- Fixed the inspector tab swap binding being undiscoverable. The chrome hint previously read `· [/] switch`, which looked like "press the `/` key", but `/` is the global filter trigger and would open the search input instead. The hint now reads `· ←/→ switch` and matches the binding.

### Maintenance

- Bumped `@langchain/anthropic` from `1.3.14` to `1.3.28`
- Bumped `@langchain/core` from `1.1.42` to `1.1.44`
- Bumped `diff` from `8.0.4` to `9.0.0`
- Bumped `release-it` (dev) from `19.0.5` to `20.0.1`

### PRs Included

**Inspector polish + boot crash recovery**

- #822 Show inspector tab header in tall mode
- #823 Always show error message on command failure
- #824 Wrap inspector tab spacer in Text to stop Ink crash
- #825 Inspector tab swap on ←/→ instead of /-shaped hint

**Dependency bumps**

- #801 Bump `@langchain/core` from 1.1.42 to 1.1.44
- #802 Bump `diff` from 8.0.4 to 9.0.0
- #803 Bump `@langchain/anthropic` from 1.3.14 to 1.3.28
- #804 Bump `release-it` (dev) from 19.0.5 to 20.0.1

### Breaking Changes

None known. The `←` / `→` binding on detail focus was previously unbound; `[` and `]` continue to work as alternates so users who memorized them are unaffected.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.40.0...0.40.1

summary: `0.40.0` makes the TUI's navigation pattern uniform across the surfaces that have nested structure. Wherever a list of items lives inside a named group (sidebar tabs containing branches and tags, the
body:
## What's Changed

`0.40.0` makes the TUI's navigation pattern uniform across the surfaces that have nested structure. Wherever a list of items lives inside a named group (sidebar tabs containing branches and tags, the status view with staged / unstaged / untracked sections, the inspector with its actions list, the stash diff with multi-file patches), the group's title is now a first-class cursor target with its own canonical action. Pressing `↑` at the top of any list escapes onto the group header; `Enter` on the header drills into the dedicated view, fires a batch action, or invokes the cursored action. The visual treatment matches across all four surfaces so the same mental model carries from one panel to the next.

## Highlights

### Three-tier sidebar navigation

- The active sidebar tab's header (Branches / Tags / Stashes / Worktrees) is now a cursor target. `↑` at item index 0 promotes the cursor onto the header; `←` / `→` scans neighboring tab headers; `Enter` on the header drills into the dedicated view (`g b` / `g t` / `g z` / `g w`).
- Header focus persists across `←` / `→` tab switches so the user can scan tab → tab → drill without re-pressing `↑`.
- Resets cleanly when focus leaves the sidebar (Tab cycles, view pushes), so a re-entry always starts on items rather than mid-flag.

### Status surface staged / unstaged / untracked groups

- The worktree status view renders files under `▾ Staged (n)` / `▾ Unstaged (n)` / `▾ Untracked (n)` headers. `←` / `→` jumps between groups (lands on the group's first file); `↑` at the first file of a group promotes onto the header.
- `Enter` on a group header fires the group's batch action: `Unstage all staged files`, `Stage all unstaged files`, or `Stage all untracked files` (with y-confirm for the destructive untracked add).
- The cursor model adapts to whichever sections are visible, so the `1` / `2` / `3` filter mask still narrows the view without breaking navigation.

### Executable inspector actions list

- The inspector's per-entity Actions cheat-sheet is now interactive. Tab to focus the inspector, `[` / `]` to toggle to the Actions tab, `↑` / `↓` to move the cursor, `Enter` to fire the cursored action.
- History-commit context wires up cherry-pick, revert, reset, interactive rebase, yank hash / short hash, open in browser, and open diff. The dispatcher emits the same events the matching keystroke would, so the action list and the keyboard stay in sync.
- The active row gets the same selection styling the sidebar header and status group header use.

### Stash diff file headers as visual anchors

- `diff --git` rows in stash diffs render as compact `▾ <path>` headers (or `> <path>` in ASCII mode) with bold + accent color, so multi-file stash patches visually segment instead of running together.
- The active file's header (the one the cursor is scrolled inside) gets the selection background. `[` / `]` continues to hop between file headers; `c` (cherry-pick), `o` (open in `$EDITOR`), and `y` (yank path) act on whatever file the cursor is currently inside.

## Notable

- **`Enter` on a sidebar tab header drills into the dedicated view.** Previously the header wasn't a cursor target; it now acts the same as pressing `g b` / `g t` / `g z` / `g w` for the active tab.
- **`Enter` on a status group header fires a batch action.** Landing on the `▾ Staged` / `▾ Unstaged` / `▾ Untracked` row via `↑` and pressing `Enter` stages or unstages every file in that group. The untracked variant requires y-confirm.
- **`Enter` is now context-routed inside the inspector.** Inspector tab focused → opens the diff for the cursored file (existing). Actions tab focused → fires the cursored action. The split is whichever inspector tab the user has visible.
- **Stash diff file headers render as `▾ <path>` instead of `diff --git a/<path> b/<path>`.** The verbose form ran together with the `index` / `---` / `+++` lines below it; the compact form makes file boundaries obvious at a glance. No information is lost because the path is the meaningful identifier.

## PRs Included

**Unified three-tier navigation pattern across surfaces** (#818, #819, #820, #821)

- #818 Sidebar tab header is a first-class cursor target
- #819 Three-tier nav for status surface (staged / unstaged / untracked groups + batch actions)
- #820 Inspector Actions list is executable
- #821 Stash diff file headers as visual anchors

## Breaking Changes

None known. The new keystroke behaviors (`Enter` on sidebar headers, `Enter` on status group headers, `Enter` on inspector actions) all activate states that didn't exist in `0.39.0`. The visual change to stash diff file headers replaces verbose dim text with shorter accented headers; no information is lost.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.39.0...0.40.0

summary: `0.39.0` is a polish release on top of the action-surface work from `0.38.0`. The cursor in one list now drives the cursor in others (cursor a branch in the sidebar and the commit log jumps to that br
body:
## What's Changed

`0.39.0` is a polish release on top of the action-surface work from `0.38.0`. The cursor in one list now drives the cursor in others (cursor a branch in the sidebar and the commit log jumps to that branch's tip), the right-hand inspector stops repeating what the header and sidebar already show and starts surfacing what's actionable on the cursored entity, and the branches list defaults to a sensible place (current branch pinned and selected). Two new commit-rooted actions land: create a branch or a lightweight tag pointing at the cursored commit without leaving the history view. The inspector itself also shrinks at rest, expands on focus, and collapses into a tabbed layout on short terminals so the commit graph keeps the spotlight when the user isn't reading detail.

## Highlights

### Cross-list cursor sync

- Cursoring a branch in the sidebar Branches tab or the dedicated branches view snaps the history panel's cursor to that branch's tip commit. Same for tags. Sidebar focus and dedicated-view focus both trigger the jump.
- The auto-jump runs through a 150ms debounce so cursor-scrolling through a long branch list with `j/k` does not redraw the history panel on every keystroke.
- A `Synced history to <ref> tip` status confirmation surfaces when the jump fires, so the user gets feedback even on the dedicated branches view where the history graph isn't visible behind the branches list.
- When the cursored ref's tip is not in the loaded commit window (common under compact `--first-parent` mode where feature-branch tips are not fetched), the status line surfaces a hint pointing the user at `\` (toggle full graph) or `Ctrl+L` (load more) rather than silently doing nothing.
- Reducer gains a `selectCommitByHash` action that locates a commit by full or short hash within `state.filteredCommits`. Resets the file index and diff offset on jump, mirroring the existing `move` semantics.

### Inspector redesign and responsive layout

- Dropped the inspector's `Workflows:` trailer that listed branches / provider / status / tags / stashes / worktrees / operation summary lines. Every entry there was already in the top header or the left sidebar, so the trailer was pure noise.
- Added an `Actions:` section at the bottom of the inspector that lists the keystrokes available on the cursored entity. History-view commits surface `c` cherry-pick, `R` revert, `Z` reset, `i` interactive rebase, `B` create branch here, `gT` create tag here, `y` / `Y` yank, `O` open in browser, `enter` open diff. Destructive entries render with a `[!]` marker and a danger-color key letter so the irreversible actions catch the eye.
- The inspector now mirrors the existing sidebar focus-expand pattern. At rest it's compact (20-32 cells, ~22% of width) so the commit graph dominates. When `tab` cycles focus to it, it expands to 36-60 cells (~40% of width) so the metadata, body preview, file list, and action panel get the room they need.
- On short terminals (rows below the threshold), the inspector collapses into a tabbed `[Inspector] Actions` layout. `[/]` while the inspector is focused cycles between the two tabs, mirroring how `[/]` cycles sidebar tabs when the sidebar is focused. Tall terminals stack both sections as before.
- Body preview bumped from 6 to 8 lines so longer commit messages render without the trailing truncation hint as often.

### Branches list defaults and checkout polish

- The current branch always pins to position 0 of the branches list, regardless of sort mode (`name` / `recent` / `ahead` all respect the pin). The cursor lands on the active branch by default since `selectedBranchIndex` was already 0.
- Falls through cleanly to plain sort under detached HEAD or fresh-clone state where no branch is marked current.
- After a successful `enter checkout`, the branches list runs a *visible* refresh (was silent) so the user sees the loading state while git updates HEAD. The cursor then snaps back to position 0, which combined with the pin rule lands it on the just-checked-out branch automatically.

### Sidebar list grows with the terminal

- The sidebar list (branches / tags / stashes / worktrees) used to cap at 8 visible items regardless of terminal height. It now grows to fill available rows: roughly `bodyRows - 10` (subtracting the panel chrome). On a 40-row terminal that's ~26 visible items under the active tab; on the 24-row minimum it stays at 8.

### Create branch / tag from cursored commit

- `B` on the history view opens a new-branch prompt. Submitting runs `git branch <name> <sha>` against the cursored commit, leaving HEAD where it is. The prompt itself is the affirmative gate, so no extra y-confirmation. Useful for marking a commit you want to come back to without disrupting your current work.
- `gT` opens a new-tag prompt and runs `git tag <name> <sha>` (lightweight). For annotated tags, the existing `+` on the tags view stays the right path; this is the per-commit shortcut.
- Tag key is the `gT` chord rather than bare `T` because bare `T` is delete-tag on the tags view. The chord mirrors the existing `gH` pattern (uppercase letter after `g`) and is distinct from lowercase `gt` which jumps to the tags view.
- Both register as palette-only `kind: 'normal'` workflow entries (`create-branch-here` / `create-tag-here`), so the inspector action list and the command palette pick them up automatically.

## Fixes

- Fixed `O` (Open in browser) on the history view opening the repo root instead of the cursored commit's URL. The handler now prefers the commit page for history-view contexts and falls back to PR / repo for everywhere else.

## Notable

- **The header label drops `ui`.** `coco ui` in the chrome top-left is now `coco`. Less redundant once the user is inside the TUI.
- **The right-hand inspector got narrower at rest.** If you had your terminal sized so the old 56-cell inspector fit exactly, the panel now floats at 20-32 cells (~22% of width). Focus the inspector via `tab` and it expands to 36-60 cells; that's where the long commit bodies and action labels render comfortably.
- **`B` is now create-branch-here on the history view.** No prior binding existed there, so this is additive, but if you had built muscle memory around `B` doing nothing it now opens an input prompt. Esc cancels.
- **The branches list reorders on first paint.** Under `name` sort, the alphabetical order you might have memorized now starts at the current branch. Under `recent` sort, the newest non-current branch is at row 1 instead of row 0.
- **`[/]` is now context-routed three ways.** Diff view: jump file/hunk. Sidebar focused: cycle sidebar tabs. Inspector focused: cycle inspector tabs (only visible on short terminals where the inspector is tabbed).

## PRs Included

**Cross-list cursor sync** (#811, #813)

**Inspector redesign + per-entity action list** (#810)

**Inspector focus-expand + dynamic sidebar height + checkout polish** (#814)

**Tabbed inspector for short terminals** (#815)

**Branches list defaults: pin current at top + default-select** (#809)

**Create branch / tag from cursored commit** (#812)

**Polish: open-in-browser commit URL, header label, cursor sync confirmation** (#813)

## Breaking Changes

None known. New keystrokes (`B` and `gT` on the history view) are scoped to that view and were unbound previously. The inspector narrowing reduces detail-panel width at rest but expands on focus so the same content still renders comfortably; the new `Actions:` and tabbed-on-short-terminals modes are additive.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.38.0...0.39.0

summary: `0.38.0` expands the TUI shell from a navigation surface into an action surface. Most of the everyday Git operations that used to require dropping back to the shell or to the GitHub web UI now live on
body:
## What's Changed

`0.38.0` expands the TUI shell from a navigation surface into an action surface. Most of the everyday Git operations that used to require dropping back to the shell or to the GitHub web UI now live one keystroke deep inside `coco ui`: revert / reset / interactive-rebase from the history view, hunk-level apply from a commit-diff or stash-diff, the full pull-request action panel (merge, close, approve, request changes, comment) for the current branch, and per-entity actions (checkout, apply, pop, drop) for whichever sidebar tab is focused. The commit graph rendering also caught up, with proper box-drawing junctions, per-lane coloring, and distinct glyphs for merges and HEAD.

## Highlights

### Higher-fidelity commit graph

- Walks `git log --graph` rows with one-char lookahead so the canonical 2-char patterns (`|\` fork, `|/` converge) render as proper box-drawing junctions (`├╮` / `├╯`) instead of the old overlapping pipes (`│╲` / `│╱`). The trunk lane gains a right-T at the junction row, so the eye can finally track which lane belongs to which branch.
- Tracks column → lane id across rows and paints each graph character in its branch's palette color. Lane ids inherit through `|` continuations, spawn fresh on `|\` forks, and absorb cleanly on `|/` converges. The palette is theme-aware and falls back to the muted graph color under `NO_COLOR` or `theme.ascii`.
- Threads `%P` (parent hashes) through the log format so every commit row carries `parents`. Merges render as `◆`, HEAD as `◉` (fisheye), and ordinary commits as `●`. HEAD wins over merge when both apply.
- Toggling graph mode (`g` in the TUI) now re-fetches with the right view, so "full graph" finally has the topology rows it needs to render. Earlier the toggle only flipped the header label and left the underlying compact rows untouched.

### Pull request action panel

- New `gp` chord opens a dedicated pull-request action panel for the current branch. Header surfaces the PR number, title, state (`OPEN · draft · mergeable` or `OPEN · conflicting · dirty`), branch arrow (`feature/x → main`), and author. Checks render as a one-line summary plus a per-check table colored by status. Reviews render with per-state counts plus the GraphQL review decision.
- Action keys scoped to the panel: `m` merge (prompts for `merge` / `squash` / `rebase`, then confirms), `x` close, `a` approve, `R` request changes (prompts for the body, then confirms), `c` comment, `O` open in browser. Each routes through a confirmation gate or an input prompt, so no irreversible action fires from a single keystroke.
- `pr-comment` and `pr-request-changes` open in multi-line mode. Pressing Enter inserts a newline, `Ctrl+D` submits. The structured prompts (branch names, merge strategies) stay single-line so muscle memory survives.
- `gh pr view --json` now requests `body`, `author`, `mergeable`, `mergeStateStatus`, `reviewDecision`, `reviews`, and `statusCheckRollup` in the same call, so the panel renders without a second round-trip.

### History view mutations

- `R` reverts the cursored commit through the y-confirm path (`git revert --no-edit <sha>`).
- `Z` opens a `soft` / `mixed` / `hard` mode prompt and resets the current branch tip to the cursored commit. `--hard` returns recovery instructions in the status line so the user always has a reflog escape hatch.
- `i` starts an interactive rebase from the cursored commit's parent. Lowercase `i` so the existing global `I` ai-commit-summary workflow stays reachable on the history view.
- All three guard against running mid-merge or mid-rebase via the existing `guardNoInProgressOperation` helper.

### Side-by-side diff toggle

- `d` on a commit-diff or stash-diff explore flips between unified and side-by-side rendering. The split view pairs `-` / `+` lines within each hunk so the eye can read removals on the left and additions on the right without mentally aligning the two halves.
- The toggle persists per-repo across sessions through the same config-cache helper used by the sidebar tab, so the user does not have to re-toggle every time.
- Falls back to unified rendering automatically on terminals narrower than 120 columns, with a one-time status hint, so the user keeps a usable diff even on a small SSH session. The user's preference is preserved for the next wide terminal.

### Hunk-level apply

- `H` inside a commit-diff or stash-diff explore extracts just the hunk under the cursor and applies it to the working tree (`git apply`). `gH` does the same against the index (`git apply --cached`).
- The patch is synthesized fresh with proper `diff --git` / `---` / `+++` headers, so the apply works even when the source view (commit-diff `filePreview.hunks`) does not include the original file headers.
- Failures from `git apply` surface on the status line with the actual git error, so the user can decide whether to fall back to a file-level cherry-pick.

### In-sidebar action surface

- The workstation sidebar's content tabs (branches, tags, stashes, worktrees) are no longer read-only previews. Cursor highlights move within the active tab as the user navigates, and per-entity actions fire without drilling into the dedicated view.
- `←` / `→` switch between sidebar tabs (status / branches / tags / stashes / worktrees). `↑` / `↓` (and `j` / `k`) navigate items inside the active tab. Empty content tabs and the status tab fall back to cycling tabs so navigation never dead-ends.
- Per-entity ops fire from the sidebar via the same dispatch as the dedicated views: Enter checkout / `D` delete / `R` rename / `u` set-upstream for branches; Enter diff / `a` apply / `p` pop / `X` drop for stashes; `T` delete / `P` push for tags; `W` remove for worktrees. The generic Enter-drill-in only fires when the focused tab has no per-entity primary action defined or has zero items.
- Per-tab footer hints surface the in-sidebar ops so the affordance is discoverable instead of hidden behind a help overlay.

### Branches list polish

- Dropped the `even with origin/<upstream>` noise that dominated every up-to-date branch row. Synced branches now show a clean line; the divergent case (`↑3 ↓1 origin/main`) still renders with the arrow form.
- Added a compact relative timestamp (`today` / `Nd` / `Nw` / `Nmo` / `Ny`) so the user can scan for "what did I touch recently" without opening a commit.
- Replaced the single-glyph row marker with a four-state indicator: `*` current, `◌` no upstream, `≡` synced with upstream, `↕` diverged from upstream. ASCII fallbacks (`?`, `=`, `~`) render cleanly on `theme.ascii` terminals.

### Stash diff header

- The stash diff panel header now shows `@{N} <message> on <branch>` instead of the raw timestamp ref (`stash@{2026-05-01 23:01:18 -0400}`). The full ref still renders inside the body so `git stash apply <ref>` is one yank away.

## Fixes

- Fixed left and right arrow keys popping the cherry-pick confirmation dialog when pressed in any view. The workflow lookup was matching empty `inputValue` against the first palette-only workflow (`cherry-pick-commit`); palette-only entries now ignore raw keystrokes outright.

## Notable

- **The graph toggle (`g`) re-fetches.** Earlier, toggling between compact and full graph mode only changed the header label and left `state.rows` as the original compact-fetched data. Now the toggle issues a fresh `git log` with the matching view flags. The first toggle on a large repo will take a moment longer than before; the result is that "full graph" finally shows the topology it always claimed to.
- **`R` is now revert on the history view, rename on the branches view.** The per-view scoping pattern keeps both reachable, but anyone with muscle memory for `R = rename` should expect to be on the branches surface (or sidebar Branches tab) before pressing it.
- **Lowercase `i` starts an interactive rebase on the history view.** The original issue suggested `I`, but `I` is taken globally by the AI commit summary workflow. Lowercase keeps both reachable and matches the `git rebase -i` flag mnemonic.
- **`Ctrl+D` submits multi-line prompts.** PR comments and review bodies now open in multi-line mode. Enter inserts a newline; submission moves to `Ctrl+D` (Unix EOF convention). Single-line prompts (branch names, merge strategies, reset modes) keep Enter as submit.

## PRs Included

**Higher-fidelity commit graph** (#792, #794, closes #791)

**Pull request action panel + multi-line prompt** (#805, #807, closes #783, #806)

**History view mutations** (#798, closes #777)

**Side-by-side diff toggle** (#799, closes #785)

**Hunk-level apply** (#800, closes #782)

**In-sidebar action surface** (#797, closes #786)

**Branches list polish** (#796)

**Stash diff header** (#795)

**Arrow key cherry-pick fix** (#793)

## Breaking Changes

None known. The new keybindings (`R` / `Z` / `i` on history; `H` / `gH` on diff; `m` / `x` / `a` / `R` / `c` on the pull-request panel; `gp` chord; `←` / `→` for sidebar tabs) are all per-view scoped so they do not collide with bindings outside their target surface. Single-line input prompts retain the original Enter-to-submit behavior.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.37.0...0.38.0

summary: `0.37.0` makes the TUI shell's busiest list views easier to drive from the keyboard. A universal yank binding copies whatever the cursor is on (commit hash, branch, tag, stash ref, file path) to the s
body:
## What's Changed

`0.37.0` makes the TUI shell's busiest list views easier to drive from the keyboard. A universal yank binding copies whatever the cursor is on (commit hash, branch, tag, stash ref, file path) to the system clipboard. The status surface gains `1`/`2`/`3` to narrow the file list to staged, unstaged, or untracked categories. The history view recognizes `path:` and `author:` filter prefixes and re-runs `git log` with the matching flags, so commits touching a path or by a given author show up without dropping to a shell. Both are quick-win affordances spun out of the post-0.36.0 review.

## Highlights

### Yank to clipboard

- `y` copies the cursored identifier to the system clipboard from every promoted view: full commit hash on history, branch shortName on branches, tag name on tags, stash ref on stash, file path on status and diff.
- `Y` yanks the short hash on the history view, and the underlying commit sha on a commit-diff explorer (handy when the user has drilled into a file but wants to share the commit reference).
- A status message confirms what was copied (`Copied commit abc1234`, `Copied branch feat/foo`). Failures to invoke the clipboard helper surface as `Copy failed: <reason>` instead of vanishing silently.
- The clipboard runner walks `pbcopy` on macOS, `clip` on Windows, and `wl-copy` / `xclip` / `xsel` on Linux, so no new dependency is required on common environments.
- The same action is reachable from the command palette as `yank`.

### Status surface filter

- `1`, `2`, and `3` toggle staged, unstaged, and untracked visibility on the status view. The mask is additive: starting from all-on, pressing one key narrows to that category; pressing more keys widens or narrows from there.
- Toggling every category off snaps the mask back to all-on rather than rendering an empty pane, so the affordance is always reversible without thinking.
- The panel header surfaces the active mask (`filter: staged + unstaged  (1/2/3 to toggle)`) when it differs from the all-on default. Stage, revert, and yank actions all key off the filtered list, so the cursor and the rendered rows stay in lock-step.

### History server-side filter

- `/path:src/commands/log` and `/author:alice` re-run the underlying `git log` with `-- <path>` and `--author=<value>` respectively. The filter input is consumed on Enter and the textual filter clears, leaving the active server-side filter visible in the history panel header.
- Subsequent typing in filter mode still substring-filters the matched commits, so a path filter and a free-text filter compose without a special syntax.
- Load-more pagination respects the active filter, so scrolling past the initial page keeps surfacing only matching commits.
- `Ctrl+U` inside filter mode now clears both the textual filter and the active server-side filter in one keystroke. Without this, an active `path:` or `author:` re-fetch would have no obvious unwind path.

## Notable

- **`1`/`2`/`3` in the status view toggle the file mask, not the sidebar tab.** From any other view (history, branches, tags, stash, diff, compose, worktrees) the keys still drive sidebar tab selection as before. Anyone in the habit of pressing a number key from the status surface to jump to a specific sidebar tab should use `[`/`]` or `g <chord>` instead.
- **Filter prefixes are scoped to the history view.** Typing `path:foo` or `author:alice` in filter mode on a non-history view falls through to a plain substring filter, matching prior behavior.

## PRs Included

**Yank cursored identifier to clipboard** (#789, closes #778)

**Status mask and history server-side filter** (#790, closes #776)

## Breaking Changes

None known. The `1`/`2`/`3` reassignment only applies while the status view has focus; sidebar tab navigation is unchanged everywhere else.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.36.0...0.37.0

summary: `0.36.0` moves the primary action of every promoted view into the TUI. `Enter` on a branch checks it out; on a stash, opens a per-file diff explorer; on a sidebar tab, drills into the matching view. `
body:
## What's Changed

`0.36.0` moves the primary action of every promoted view into the TUI. `Enter` on a branch checks it out; on a stash, opens a per-file diff explorer; on a sidebar tab, drills into the matching view. `c` cherry-picks a commit or a single file, with a `y`/`n` confirmation on every path that can overwrite the worktree. `+` creates a branch or tag from the sidebar, `P` pushes a tag, `O` opens the current PR in the browser, and `o` opens the cursored file in `$EDITOR`. The sidebar gains an accordion layout with per-tab status counts, grows when focused, and remembers its tab per repo. A new worktrees promoted view joins branches, tags, and stash.

## Highlights

### Sidebar

- The sidebar is now an accordion: each tab shows a color-coded status count, so the size of what's waiting (dirty files, branches, tags, stashes, worktrees) is visible without drilling in.
- Focused sidebars grow to fit long branch and file names. At rest the sidebar stays compact and the diff and history panels keep most of the width.
- `Enter` on a tab opens the matching promoted view (status, branches, tags, stashes, worktrees) and moves the cursor straight into the new list.
- `+` from the sidebar (or the matching promoted view) opens an inline text prompt to create a new branch or tag.
- The active tab persists per repo. Reopening a project lands on whichever tab was last in use there.

### Branches, tags, and stash

- `Enter` on a branch checks it out.
- `Enter` on a stash row opens a per-file stash diff explorer. `]` and `[` jump between files. `c` cherry-picks the file under the cursor.
- `a` applies the selected stash, `p` pops it, and `X` drops it after confirmation.
- `P` on the tags view pushes the selected tag to origin. `T` deletes a tag locally with confirmation. A separate palette workflow handles remote-tag deletion.
- Non-destructive workflows now run directly when invoked from the keyboard or the command palette. Previously palette selection set internal state but never fired the action.

### Cherry-pick

- `c` on the history view cherry-picks the selected commit on top of the current branch, after a `y`/`n` confirmation.
- `c` on a commit-diff or stash-diff explorer cherry-picks the cursored file. Both routes share the same confirmation flow.
- File deletions are mirrored: cherry-picking a deleted file now runs `git rm` instead of erroring out, so the action works for additions, modifications, and deletions equally.

### Worktrees

- New worktrees promoted view, reachable from the sidebar (`Enter` on the Worktrees tab) and the `g w` chord.
- `W` removes the selected linked worktree after confirmation.
- The action refuses to operate on the current worktree and resolves its target against the visible filtered list, so a hidden row can never be the target.

### Repo and editor

- `O` opens the current branch's pull request in the browser. When no PR is detected, falls back to the repo page.
- `o` opens the file under the cursor in `$VISUAL` or `$EDITOR` (status, worktree diff, stash diff). Common configurations such as `code -w`, `vim -f`, and `emacs -nw` are tokenized so they actually launch.
- An editor killed by a signal (for example `^C` or SIGTERM) is now reported as an interruption. Previously the TUI silently treated it as a successful edit.

### Status, polish, reliability

- Status messages auto-dismiss after a few seconds. Modals (input prompts, confirmations, palette) suppress the timer while open so live feedback for the open task isn't cleared mid-flow.
- The compose pane drops the AI prompt nag when there's nothing to summarize, anchors the stateline, defaults the sidebar to Branches, and wraps both summary and body so long text no longer clips at the panel edge.
- Diff-view footer hints adapt to the diff source (worktree, commit, stash). The keys advertised match what actually fires.
- The center diff column keeps a stable width across resizes. Background watcher refreshes are silent, so the screen no longer flashes on every disk event.
- Cherry-pick (commit and file), tag delete, branch delete, drop-stash, remove-worktree, abort-operation, and AI-summary actions all route through the same `y`/`n` confirmation panel before mutating anything.

## Fixes

- Fixed file-level cherry-picks bypassing the destructive-action confirmation. A single `c` on a commit-diff or stash-diff file could overwrite uncommitted edits to that path.
- Fixed file-level cherry-picks erroring out silently for deletions. The action now mirrors the deletion in the worktree.
- Fixed the worktrees view's cursor desyncing under an active filter. ↑/↓ counted unfiltered rows, so the selection could advance past visible items.
- Fixed sidebar persistence overwriting the user's chosen tab whenever they pushed compose or status. The auto-switch to Branches no longer leaks into per-repo storage.
- Fixed mount-time tab restore stealing focus into the sidebar. The cursor now stays on commits while the saved tab is shown in the gutter.
- Fixed the stash diff opening at the previous diff's scroll offset instead of the top of the patch.
- Fixed `parseStashDiffFiles` missing files whose paths contained spaces or special characters. Git's quoted-path form (`"a/path with spaces"`) is now parsed with C-style escape decoding.
- Fixed the command palette dispatching non-confirm workflows as a no-op.
- Fixed stale branches and tags footer hints, duplicate staged-files entries after a successful commit, and the post-commit status message lingering on reset.
- Fixed destructive workflow actions wired only as setters; they now invoke their underlying handler.

## Notable

- **`c` is destructive on every surface now.** All three cherry-pick paths (history commit, commit-diff file, stash-diff file) require `y`/`n` confirmation. The two file-level paths previously fired immediately, which could discard uncommitted edits.
- **Sidebar tab is per-repo.** Marker file lives at `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/coco/sidebar-tab.<hash>`, falling back to `~/.cache/coco/`. Switching projects no longer resets every other repo's preference.
- **`W` no longer falls back to "first non-current."** Pressing `W` while the cursor is on the current worktree refuses the action with a message. Previously it silently deleted some other worktree.

## PRs Included

**TUI promoted-view actions and post-review correctness pass** (#788). Single umbrella PR. The cluster covers: sidebar accordion with status counts, focus-driven sidebar growth, `Enter` from the sidebar into promoted views, `+` create-branch / create-tag from the sidebar, per-repo tab persistence, branch `Enter` checkout, tag `P` push, stash `Enter` / `a` / `p` and the per-file diff explorer, the new worktrees view and `W`, full-commit and file-level cherry-pick, `O` open PR or repo, `o` open file in editor, the generic text-input prompt, status auto-dismiss, compose pane polish, contextual diff-view footer hints, palette workflow dispatch, panel-width stability, silent watcher refresh, and the post-review pass that gated cherry-picks behind `y`/`n`, mirrored deletions, fixed sidebar persistence and focus regressions, handled quoted stash paths, and tightened the worktree action targeting.

## Breaking Changes

None known. The cherry-pick `c` change tightens an existing path with a confirmation prompt rather than removing functionality. Anyone who scripted around the unconfirmed file-level variant should expect the prompt now.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.35.0...0.36.0

summary: `0.35.0` builds polish and reliability on top of the navigation shell that landed in `0.34.0`. The TUI gains chord, onboarding, and mode-badge cues for discoverability; live refresh on `.git` and work
body:
## What's Changed

`0.35.0` builds polish and reliability on top of the navigation shell that landed in `0.34.0`. The TUI gains chord, onboarding, and mode-badge cues for discoverability; live refresh on `.git` and working-tree activity; iconography across branches, files, and PRs; a graph rewrite with colored row spans and Unicode topology; OSC 8 hyperlinks and truecolor; a preview pane and sort toggles for branches, tags, and stash; and a commit-diff explore mode. A new `coco doctor` command reports which config source provides each setting, and `.coco.json` joins `.coco.config.json` as a project config filename.

## Highlights

### Discoverable navigation

- Pressing `g` now opens a which-key chord overlay listing every available second-key continuation (`h`/`s`/`d`/`c`/`b`/`t`/`z`/`g`) with descriptions until the chord is completed or cancelled. The footer mirrors the same hints inline.
- New users see a one-time first-launch overlay that orients them to the most important keys before the TUI opens. The marker file lives alongside the rest of the XDG cache.
- TUI crashes and `ctrl+z` backgrounds restore the terminal cleanly via panic and suspend handlers, instead of leaving an orphaned alt-screen.
- A header mode badge surfaces the active mode (normal, filter, help, palette).
- After a few seconds of inactivity, a status-line tip rotates a context-relevant hint.

### Richer signal at a glance

- Iconography lands across the TUI: divergence arrows (`↑n`/`↓n`) on branches, PR state glyphs (open, merged, closed, draft), stage dots (`●` staged, `○` unstaged, `?` untracked) on file rows, and badge counts on every sidebar tab.
- The history graph renders with colored row spans and Unicode topology characters (`├`, `╰`, `╭`, `│`). ASCII fallback preserves correct column alignment when glyphs are unavailable.
- OSC 8 terminal hyperlinks make commit hashes, PR refs, and remote URLs clickable in capable terminals (iTerm2, kitty, Wezterm, Terminal.app).
- Truecolor rendering with graceful 256-color and ANSI-16 fallbacks. `NO_COLOR` continues to be honored end-to-end.

### Live, responsive UI

- The TUI watches `.git/` and the working tree and updates history, status, and branch state without a manual refresh. Disk activity is debounced so renders stay smooth.
- A synthetic `(+) new commit` row appears at the top of history when the working tree is dirty, putting the diff and compose flow in the same column as historical commits.
- Applying a filter preserves the cursor when the selected item is still in the filtered set, instead of jumping back to the top.
- Center panel layout is stable across terminal resizes; the diff column no longer reflows.

### Branches, tags, and stash explorer

- A preview pane on the branches, tags, and stash views updates the right-hand panel with the selection, no drill-in required.
- `s` cycles sort modes for branches and tags (date, name, ahead/behind).

### Compose and diff

- Commit-diff explore mode drills into a commit's file list, pages through hunks with `j`/`k`, and scrolls diffs with `pgup`/`pgdn`. The same shell is reused for worktree diffs so the keymap is identical.
- A line-scroll diff navigation mode lands alongside colored diff output and a loading indicator, so long AI generations stop feeling frozen.
- Entering compose from history now follows the worktree state, not the selected commit's tree.
- AI draft generation guards against empty and oversize diffs that previously could throw mid-render.

### Diagnostics and configuration

- `coco doctor` reports which config source (CLI flag, env var, project, git, XDG, or default) is providing each setting, plus provider availability checks and commitlint config detection.
- `.coco.json` is the preferred project config filename. `.coco.config.json` continues to work without changes.

## Fixes

- Fixed commit-diff hunk navigation running off the end of the diff instead of clamping at file boundaries.
- Fixed the compose surface clipping summary and body text instead of wrapping at the panel edge.
- Fixed destructive workflow actions (cherry-pick, push-tag, delete-branch) skipping the confirmation pipeline.
- Fixed background watcher refreshes flashing the screen on every disk event.
- Fixed the staged-files list duplicating entries after a successful commit and not clearing the post-commit status message on reset.
- Fixed the `coco doctor` handler so it typechecks under strict mode and exits cleanly via the shared command executor.
- Fixed the commit split handler not wiring interactive mode and its callback when invoked from `coco commit`.

## Notable

- **Footer hints adapt per view and per mode.** Anyone scripting around the previous single-line footer should expect a contextual-left and globals-right split anchored across all surfaces.
- **First-launch onboarding marker.** The marker file is created in the XDG cache directory the first time the TUI opens in 0.35.0. To re-trigger the overlay, delete the marker file.

## PRs Included

**TUI shell polish** (#763 graph polish, #764 discoverability, #765 consistency, #766 iconography, #767 preview pane, #768 sort and idle tips, #769 selection clamp, #770 hyperlinks and truecolor)

**Live data and pending commit row** (#761, #762)

**Compose and diff** (#758, #759, #760)

**Doctor and config** (#771, #772)

**Reliability follow-ups** (#773, #774, #775)

## Breaking Changes

None known.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gfargo/coco/compare/0.34.0...0.35.0

summary: 0.34.0 transforms `coco log -i` and `coco ui` from "interactive prompts on top of a log command" into a **cohesive, navigable TUI** for daily git operations.  Every git surface — history, working tree
body:
## What's Changed                                                                                                                                               

0.34.0 transforms `coco log -i` and `coco ui` from "interactive prompts on top of a log command" into a **cohesive, navigable TUI** for daily git operations.  Every git surface — history, working tree, branches, tags, stash, compose — is reachable from every other one through a unified navigation stack, a chord-based keymap, and an interactive command palette.                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                  
It also rolls up a substantial set of dependency upgrades (langchain v1 majors, langchain/core, inquirer v8, others) and several smaller reliability fixes.       
   
  ## Highlights                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                  
  ### TUI shell — every git surface, one keystroke away                                                                                                             
   
  `coco ui` and `coco log -i` now share a real navigation system. From any view, you can jump anywhere with a `g`-prefixed chord, pop back with `<` or `esc`, and   
  pull up any command by name with `:`.                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Global navigation chords** (work from any view):                                                                                                                
   
  - `g h` — history                                                                                                                                                 
  - `g s` — status                                          
  - `g d` — diff                                                                                                                                                    
  - `g c` — compose                                         
  - `g b` — branches
  - `g t` — tags
  - `g z` — stash
  - `<` or `esc` — back through the navigation stack
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Contextual transitions:**
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - `enter` on a commit in history opens its diff (selection follows you)                                                                                           
  - `enter` on a file in status opens its diff (back returns to status)
  - `e` / `c` from status or diff push compose with the appropriate state                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Persistent shell chrome:**                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Header shows a `›`-separated breadcrumb so you always know where you are in the navigation stack.                                                               
  - Footer splits into contextual hints on the left and persistent globals (`g jump · < back · ? help · : cmds · q quit`) anchored to the right.
  - Help overlay is grouped into `Global` and `This view (...)` sections, labelled with the active view so the right scope is always obvious.                       
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Promoted top-level views:**                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - **Compose** — full-screen draft editor with summary/body cursors, AI-draft trigger, hook/validation feedback, and a compact staged-files summary. Draft state   
  persists across navigation — round-trip through history and back never drops your message.
  - **Branches**, **tags**, and **stash** — first-class lists with selection cursors, sliding viewports, and per-view selection that survives navigation.           
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Interactive command palette (`:`)**:                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Fuzzy-matches against label, description, keys, and id.                                                                                                         
  - Recently-used items float to the top when the filter is empty.
  - Lists every keybinding *and* every workflow action (`commit`, `delete-branch`, `ai-commit-summary`, etc.) under one searchable surface.                         
  - Up/down or `ctrl+p`/`ctrl+n` move the cursor; `enter` runs; `esc` closes.                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Global search:**                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - The existing `/` filter mode now applies to branches, tags, and stash views in addition to history.                                                             
   
  **Polish:**                                                                                                                                                       
                                                            
  - Empty states across every view are tailored: no commits, clean working tree, no branches, no tags, no stashes, no staged changes — each gets a hint pointing at 
  the next sensible action (e.g. `gs` to stage, `gc` to come back to compose).
  - Loading lines unified to `· Loading <resource>…`.                                                                                                               
  - `NO_COLOR` is honored end-to-end; layout is ASCII-only so a missing glyph never breaks columns. Monochrome theme is the canary for layout correctness without   
  text decoration.                                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                    
  ### Interactive log TUI — `coco log -i`                                                                                                                           
                                                            
  The Ink-based interactive log itself shipped over ~30 PRs across the cycle. Highlights:                                                                           
                                                            
  - Branch overview, branch actions, branch prompts, PR workflows.                                                                                                  
  - Tag management and status/staging.                      
  - Commit workflows: stage/unstage hunks, amend, reword, split — all going through the same pipeline as the non-interactive `commit` command.                      
  - Stash and worktree workflows, hunk staging, history actions, validation/hook feedback.                                                                          
  - AI workflows surfaced in-TUI: provider-aware integrations, async context hydration so the UI starts instantly while data streams in.                            
  - Search/navigation polish, theming, detail previews. Honors `NO_COLOR` and ASCII-only terminals.                                                                 
  - Default commit-display limit raised; lazy "load more" for older commits.                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                    
  ### `coco ui` — the daily-driver entry point                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                    
  A new top-level command built on the log-TUI runtime, designed to be the primary interactive surface long-term.                                                   
                                                            
  - Foundation, layout shell, surface composition.                                                                                                                  
  - Worktree-aware diff view.                               
  - Wired-up staging actions and a commit-compose flow.                                                                                                             
  - Higher-fidelity history graph rendering shared with the log TUI.                                                                                                
  - See `coco ui --help` and the wiki docs for current scope.                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                    
  ### LangChain v1 migration                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - `@langchain/core` `1.1.34` → `1.1.42`                                                                                                                           
  - `@langchain/community` `1.1.23` → `1.1.27`              
  - `@langchain/openai` `0.6.17` → `1.4.5` (major)                                                                                                                  
  - `@langchain/ollama` `0.2.4` → `1.2.7` (major)                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                    
  The two majors required a small change to schema generation: `service.fields` for the OpenAI and Ollama services is now described as a generic open object instead
   of inlining `Partial<OpenAIInput>` / `Partial<OllamaInput>`. `service.fields` is still passed through to the underlying LangChain client verbatim — runtime      
  behavior is unchanged. Net effect: `schema.json` shrinks by ~2,500 lines and is no longer coupled to upstream type churn.                                         
                                                            
  ### Documentation                                                                                                                                                 
   
  - Stable docs migrated to the [GitHub wiki](https://github.com/gfargo/coco/wiki); root README and command-level docs are now concise pointers.                    
  - New wiki maintenance workflow for keeping wiki and code in sync.
  - Updated guidance for `coco log -i` and the new `coco ui` command.                                                                                               
  - New consolidated [TUI Navigation](https://github.com/gfargo/coco/wiki/TUI-Navigation) wiki page covering the chord set, navigation stack, palette mechanics,    
  search, help overlay, themes, and accessibility.                                                                                                                  
  - Top-of-file accessibility/theming doc block in `inkRuntime.ts` capturing TUI design conventions (keyboard-only, `NO_COLOR`, ASCII layout, theme presets,        
  empty/loading/error expectations).                                                                                                                                
                                                            
  ### Reliability and Maintenance                                                                                                                                   
                                                            
  - Improved fidelity of non-interactive `coco log` stdout output.                                                                                                  
  - Replaced direct `process.exit` calls in command handlers with structured exits.
  - Shared diff parser option construction across commands for consistency.                                                                                         
  - Inquirer prompts upgraded to v8.                                                                                                                                
  - Routine dependency bumps: `rollup`, `@rollup/plugin-eslint`, `@commitlint/core`, `@types/node`.                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                    
  ## Notable                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                    
  - **`g` is now a pure chord prefix.** Pressing `g` alone no longer does anything immediately — it waits for the second key (`g h`/`g s`/`g d`/`g c`/`g b`/`g t`/`g
   z`/`g g`). This was a deliberate trade: previously `g` did `toggleGraph` as a side effect, which caused a brief graph flicker on every `g`-prefixed chord.
  **`toggleGraph` moved from `g` to `\`** — visible in the help overlay (`?`) and the command palette (`:`).                                                        
  - **Schema definitions for `service.fields`** (OpenAI/Ollama) are now open objects. If you were relying on the previous schema to validate LangChain-internal
  callback options in your config, those keys are no longer validated at the schema level — they are still accepted at runtime exactly as before.                   
   
  ## PRs Included                                                                                                                                                   
                                                            
  **Pre-TUI groundwork** (#657, #669, #670, #671)                                                                                                                   
   
  **Documentation** (#673, #674, #755, plus wiki updates for `Coco-UI`, `Interactive-Log-TUI`, `Command-Reference`, `Home`, and a new `TUI-Navigation` page)        
                                                            
  **Interactive log TUI build-out** (#675, #676, #682–#695, #709–#712, #717–#723)                                                                                   
                                                            
  **`coco ui` command** (#732–#737, #739)                                                                                                                           
                                                            
  **TUI shell epic** (#747 epic, closed by these phases):                                                                                                           
  - #748 phase 1 — navigation primitives & view stack       
  - #749 phase 2 — persistent shell chrome                                                                                                                          
  - #750 phase 3 — cross-view keybindings & contextual transitions
  - #751 phase 4 — compose as a top-level view                                                                                                                      
  - #752 phase 5 — branches/tags/stash promoted to top-level views                                                                                                  
  - #753 phase 6 — interactive command palette & global search                                                                                                      
  - #754 phase 7 — polish (empty/loading states + a11y notes)                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                    
  **Dependency bumps**:

summary: This release continues the push toward making Coco a more reliable, observable, and workflow-oriented Git assistant. It adds new commit-splitting workflows, dynamic model routing, better LLM call visi
body:
## What's Changed

This release continues the push toward making Coco a more reliable, observable, and workflow-oriented Git assistant. It adds new commit-splitting workflows, dynamic model routing, better LLM call visibility, stronger prompt budget controls, expanded CI coverage, and the first version of a visual git log command.

## Highlights

### Visual Git Log

- Added `coco log`, a new command for exploring commit history with graph/table output, JSON output, filters, and single-commit changed-file details.
- Supports common history filters including branch/ref, author, date range, path, merge exclusion, and result limits.
- Added packaged CLI smoke coverage for `coco log --help`.

### Commit Splitting

- Added commit split planning and apply workflows for breaking large staged changes into smaller related commits.
- Added hunk-level split support so related changes inside the same file can be separated into distinct commits.
- Added documentation for the commit split workflow.

### Smarter AI Model Routing

- Added dynamic model routing profiles so Coco can use different configured models for different tasks.
- Expanded coverage for dynamic model behavior across commit and summarization paths.
- Added docs for configuring dynamic routing.

### LLM Cost, Reliability, and Observability

- Added per-command LLM telemetry summaries to make AI usage easier to understand and audit.
- Added an AI call audit document covering where remote model calls happen and where future optimization is possible.
- Enforced prompt budgets for recap and review flows.
- Added large-repo and large-diff budget stress coverage.

### CI/CD and Release Confidence

- Added a Node release matrix for the earliest supported Node version, `22.12.0`, and current Node `24.x`.
- Added packaged CLI smoke checks to verify the built package works after install.
- Added release dry-run validation to catch release-time failures before publishing.
- Added branch protection documentation and stronger release gate coverage.

### Security and Maintenance

- Upgraded `simple-git` to reduce known dependency vulnerabilities.
- Added temp git repo integration coverage for command-level workflows.
- Added a v1 command reliability audit with follow-up issues for review coverage, shared diff-to-prompt pipelines, command exit handling, and init smoke paths.

## PRs Included

- #608 Security upgrade for `simple-git`
- #625 Release gate and Node matrix CI
- #626 Large diff condensation stress coverage
- #627 Temp git repo command integration coverage
- #628 LLM call observability and AI call audit
- #629 Dynamic model routing profiles
- #630 Commit split planning and apply workflow
- #638 Hunk-level commit split support
- #639 Packaged CLI smoke checks
- #640 Release dry-run validation
- #641 Dynamic model routing coverage
- #642 Recap and review prompt budget enforcement
- #643 Per-command LLM telemetry summaries
- #644 Large-repo budget coverage
- #655 v1 command reliability audit
- #656 Visual git log command

## Breaking Changes

None known.

summary: This release improves the reliability and cost profile of `coco commit` and `coco changelog`, especially for large diffs and branches with substantial change history.
body:
## Changelog

This release improves the reliability and cost profile of `coco commit` and `coco changelog`, especially for large diffs and branches with substantial change history.

### Highlights

- Avoid unnecessary LLM summarization when raw diffs already fit within the configured token budget.
- Limit concurrent `git diff` collection to prevent large staged changes from spawning too many git subprocesses at once.
- Route changelog diff modes through the shared diff condensation pipeline so changelog prompts receive real, budget-aware patch context.
- Enforce the fully rendered prompt token budget before model calls, accounting for prompt instructions, branch context, commitlint rules, previous commits, and additional context.
- Improve pre-commit hook failure handling with cleaner errors and interactive retry/skip options.

### Fixes

- Fixed `changelog --only-diff` sending metadata-only JSON instead of actual patch content.
- Fixed `changelog --with-diff` bypassing diff condensation and sending full commit patches directly to the model.
- Fixed large-file pre-summarization running before total diff budget was known.
- Fixed unbounded concurrent diff collection during staged file parsing.
- Added a deterministic prompt-budget fallback that trims summary content when overhead pushes the final prompt over budget.

### Maintenance

- Removed `package-lock.json` from the repository since the project uses Yarn.
- Updated dependency and dev dependency versions, including `@commitlint/core`, `@commitlint/types`, `diff`, `langsmith`, `jest`, `ts-jest`, and Rollup-related packages.
